plan:
# Integrated Loader Redesign — Loader Authority Artifact with Declared-Load-Closure Enforcement Model (Clotho plan delta, spec v2.8 → v2.9)

## 0. Integration thesis

This spec descends from BOTH sources. The CONSTRAINTS source's Loader Authority Artifact (LAA) is adopted verbatim as the normative artifact, schema, trust-boundary table, failure semantics, and all ten LOAD-INV obligations — none dropped, none weakened. The IMPLEMENTATION source's Declared Load Closure (DLC) contributes the realized smaller model: one artifact read two ways (capability bound + provenance), one gate check in the existing check-registry, positive closed-set membership discipline, the two-level builtin bound realized as confined non-loader service units, and the net-negative behavioral ledger.

One genuine tension between the sources is surfaced explicitly rather than blended (§8): the implementation seat's gate-only posture ("RUNTIME: nothing — no hook, no interception") versus LOAD-INV-2/LOAD-INV-3's requirement that the effective resolver domain AT LOAD TIME equal `u.imports` exactly and that generated code (`new Function('s','return import(s)')(...)`) be denied at the semantic load boundary — with source-pattern recognition explicitly non-evidence, which rules out discharging F3-generated by static extraction alone. The integrated design resolves what CAN be resolved without weakening either contract: it keeps the gate as the sole VALIDATION point (implementation seat), and mediates the SEMANTIC LOAD BOUNDARY inside the gate's own execution of the weaver via a bounded compartment (constraints seat) built exclusively from Node ≥ 18 primitives with zero runtime dependencies (`vm.SourceTextModule` linked over the verified byte buffers, with the linker's domain constructed to equal the validated graph — a construction argument, not a scanner). This is NOT a general-purpose runtime loader hook: the linker's possible target domain per unit is exactly `u.imports`, so by the constraints source's own definition it is not a general-purpose loader; it is the positive authority construction LOAD-INV-3 demands. The implementation seat's prohibition is preserved in its substance: no `--loader` flag, no `module.register`, no import maps, no monkey-patching, no ambient-resolver interception, no runtime dependency. IF The Eye rules that even this bounded gate-internal linker violates the implementation seat's frame, the conflict routes `terminal: needs-eye` per §5.7 of the constraints source — it is NOT resolvable by restoring a form scanner or accepting throw-after-execution as denial.

## 1. Normative model (constraints source, preserved exactly)

The four existing loader mechanisms (AM-28 outbound loader-capability form scanner; AM-31/AM-33 `LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS` as loader permission; source-derived accepted relative module-load closure; AM-34 shared-recognizer coupling) are replaced by the **Loader Authority Artifact (LAA)**: one finite, canonical, content-addressed declaration per weaver of the entry unit, every executable unit it may load, and every permitted direct load edge. Absence of an edge is denial. No prose, configuration, wildcard, prefix, regex, default, inferred closure, or native-resolution fallback grants anything. All definitions of the constraints source §1 (unit, load request, direct capability, transitive capability, weaver provenance, general-purpose loader, weaver compartment) are adopted unmodified. A source parser retains only its ordinary language role; Clotho MUST NOT use it to enumerate or classify loader forms — enforcement is against actual authority and resolution.

## 2. Artifact schema (constraints source §3, normative; supersedes DLC §1.2 manifest)

Media type UTF-8 JSON; schema literal `telos.clotho.loader-authority/v1`. Top-level fields exactly: `schema`, `weaver` (frozen registry ID), `runtime` ({`nodeVersion`,`moduleAbi`} exact disk-ground-truth values), `entry` (unit key), `units` (nonempty). Unit records exactly: `key` (`^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{0,63}$`, unique), `path` (canonical repo-relative POSIX, unique, no `.`/`..`/empty segments, no backslash/absolute prefix/symlink traversal), `sourceSha256` (`sha256:` + 64 lowercase hex), `format` (`esm`), `imports` (array of {`request`: nonempty NFC string, no NUL, no `*`, exact literal, unique per unit; `target`: unit key in same LAA}; empty array explicit and valid). No target kind exists for builtins, packages, arbitrary files, URLs, data URLs, remote resources, or external manifests — such functionality crosses a separately justified non-loader service boundary (§5) and cannot become a module target. All units reachable from `entry` (unreachable = surplus authority = reject); cycles permitted only with all edges explicit. Canonical bytes: RFC 8785 JCS, NFC strings, `units` sorted by `key`, `imports` sorted by (`request`,`target`); noncanonical input bytes rejected. LAA identity = `sha256:` + lowercase SHA-256 of canonical bytes, external to the artifact. Module identity in provenance = (LAA digest, unit key, sourceSha256); paths are locators, not identities.

DLC deltas absorbed: the manifest IS the LAA (colocated `<weaver>.laa.json`, covered by the same provenance discipline); the DLC `builtins` field is REMOVED from the schema per the constraints source — builtin functionality is realized per §5 as confined units/inert data with no loader authority, not as declared builtin edges. The DLC per-module `relative` edge list becomes `imports` request→target records. The DLC's exact-by-schema/F1-unrepresentability property is preserved and strengthened by JCS canonicalization + strict-schema rejection.

## 3. Gate check `loader-authority` (implementation seat's single-check model, realized)

One check-registry entry replaces all four mechanisms. Pure, reads disk, no side effects beyond verdict; `{ok:true, closure: VerifiedGraph} | {ok:false, finding}` where `finding` cites the first violated LOAD-INV obligation ID. Pipeline, fail-closed at every step, all validation completing BEFORE any weaver unit executes:

1. **Read & canonicalize LAA from disk** (distrust submission): parse, reject duplicate JSON keys, reserialize via JCS, byte-compare, compute digest. Unknown schema versions, unknown fields, noncanonical encoding → reject (LOAD-INV-1, LOAD-INV-6).
2. **Structural graph validation**: schema closure, key/path uniqueness, path canonicality and containment under the approved root, `entry` membership, dangling targets, duplicate requests, reachability of every unit from `entry` (LOAD-INV-1, LOAD-INV-5, LOAD-INV-6).
3. **Runtime binding**: `nodeVersion`/`moduleAbi` equal disk-ground-truth runtime values; mismatch → reject (LOAD-INV-6).
4. **Byte pinning**: for every unit, resolve the path with symlink-refusing traversal (`O_NOFOLLOW`-equivalent per component via `lstat` walk), confirm regular file, read bytes ONCE into an immutable buffer, verify sha256. Execution consumes THESE buffers — no reopen-by-path after validation, closing the TOCTOU race (LOAD-INV-5).
5. **Bounded compartment construction & execution** (the semantic load boundary; the construction argument for LOAD-INV-2/3): the gate instantiates each unit as a `vm.SourceTextModule` over its verified buffer inside a compartment context whose globals are exactly: inert ECMAScript intrinsics (frozen), plus explicitly approved inert values/service endpoints per §5. The link callback resolves a request from unit `u` by EXACT literal lookup in `u.imports`; hit → the pre-instantiated module for the mapped target (its verified bytes, nothing else); miss → immediate `RunInvalid` — never normalized, never passed to native resolution, no package/URL/filesystem/ambient fallback. `importModuleDynamically` uses the SAME per-unit map, so a declared dynamic request resolves only to its mapped target and an undeclared one dies before linking. The compartment context contains no `require`, no `module`, no `process`, no host loader callback, and no route whose transitive reachable authority exceeds the calling unit's edges; `createRequire` is unreachable because `node:module` is not a possible target kind and the TCB's own capabilities do not cross the boundary. Generated code (`eval`/`new Function`) inside the compartment either has code generation unavailable (context option) or, if available, its `import()` routes through the same bounded dynamic-import handler — denial is by authority, not by recognizing the generating source. This discharges F3-generated/aliased, require-style, dynamic, side-effect, URL/data/absolute/bare/builtin specifier, and ambient-acquisition cases WITHOUT any form recognizer.
6. **Atomicity**: any denial after execution begins invalidates the ENTIRE run; the run-record is staged and published only on a fully clean verdict; rejected runs publish no evidence, provenance, or success state (LOAD-INV-6, LOAD-INV-10).

Explicitly retired from the DLC design per the constraints source: the `extractImportSurface` static extraction routine and extracted==declared set-equality check. That routine — even framed as a closed ACCEPTED grammar — determines authority by recognizing source syntax, which LOAD-INV-2 names as non-evidence and grounds for refusal, and its wholesale rejection of dynamic `import(` is a form-triggered oracle the constraints source forbids (F3 requires denial "because the bounded load boundary denies the request," not pattern presence). The implementation seat's INTENT behind the extractor — that authority be decided by declaration membership, never by dangerous-form taxonomy — is fully preserved: the linker's exact-literal edge map IS declaration membership, enforced at the point of actual resolution. The DLC's exact-equality (no over-declaration) property survives as the reachability rule: every unit must be reachable from entry (no surplus authority) and provenance is exactly entry + reachability (no over-attribution).

## 4. Provenance = the same object (DLC §1.5, realized under LOAD-INV-4)

The recorded provenance value is the validated LAA digest. The module projection is entry + graph reachability, computed from the one validated immutable graph object that also feeds enforcement — one artifact, two reads, one derivation. `orchestrator_refs`, if retained for compatibility, carries ONLY the LAA digest under a new exact schema meaning; a copied module closure there is schema-invalid. Consumers MUST dereference and validate the addressed LAA; cached/caller-supplied lists are non-authoritative. Changing any edge changes the canonical bytes, hence the digest, hence the provenance identity — F2 structurally foreclosed. AM-34's coupling obligation retires with its object: with one derivation there is nothing to couple.

## 5. Least-authority preservation of AM-33 legitimate uses (LOAD-INV-7; supersedes DLC `BUILTIN_EXPORTS_PERMITTED`)

The DLC's frozen `BUILTIN_EXPORTS_PERMITTED` constant is NOT adopted as a builtin-edge grant mechanism: the constraints schema admits no builtin module target, and a standing spec-level export allowlist would re-create mechanism #2 under a new name (an LOAD-INV-8 orphan-authority path). The implementation seat's underlying goals — exact-pair admission instead of blanket prohibition, and preservation of `builtinModules`/`isBuiltin` — are realized as the constraints source requires: each preserved AM-33 member becomes either (a) an inert generated-data unit (e.g., the frozen builtin-name list emitted at build time as a hashed, imports-empty ESM data unit inside the LAA graph — `isBuiltin` becomes membership lookup in inert data), or (b) a confined non-loader service value injected at compartment initialization, covered by the positive authority argument, structurally unable to return or manufacture loader authority (return values are structured-cloned/frozen primitives and plain data; no functions, namespaces, or host callbacks cross inward). The integrator produces the complete AM-33 disposition table — exactly one disposition per member of the exact frozen mapping, no `e.g.`, no ellipses, no categorical remainder — with artifact digests and equivalence tests for every preserved member and usage-absence evidence for every removed member. If any member cannot be preserved without exposing general loader authority → `terminal: needs-eye`, not allowlist restoration.

## 6. Complete retirement (LOAD-INV-8) and net-negative surface (LOAD-INV-9)

Retirement matrix: (1) form scanner → deleted; replaced by bounded-resolver authority semantics (inversion preserved from DLC: no dangerous-form list survives, and no accepted-form extractor replaces it either); (2) `LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS` → deleted as permission mechanism; legitimate members migrate per §5 disposition table; (3) source-derived accepted relative closure → deleted; the LAA graph is the only closure; (4) shared-recognizer coupling (AM-34) → retired as objectless. Repository search evidence (constants, branches, schema fields, test oracles, doc rules, dormant fallbacks) plus control-flow evidence that no orphan authority path remains. Historical loader spellings survive ONLY inside black-box adversarial tests asserting semantic confinement; the test corpus is declared non-normative and MUST NOT become a recognized-form definition.

Behavioral-delta ledger (DLC §4 methodology, recounted for the integrated model): RETIRED 14 rules (scanner: 7 — createRequire/aliased/namespace/default/bare-vs-node-prefix recognition, ambiguity fail-closed, per-form widening; allowlist: 3 — membership rule, AM-31 constructed-loader prohibition, AM-33 freeze amendment; separate provenance derivation: 2 — independent closure algorithm, its specifier-form handling; AM-34 coupling: 2 — same-recognizer and drift-detection obligations). INTRODUCED 6 rules: LAA canonical-schema validity (incl. JCS/digest) — 1; disk byte-pinning + path/symlink discipline — 1; graph validation (reachability, dangling, duplicates) — 1; exact-edge resolution semantics (miss = deny, no fallback) — 1; positive compartment initialization bound — 1; atomic run invalidation — 1. Net −8 across semantic distinctions, validation branches, authority stores (4→1), failure modes (unified into one fail-closed enumeration with one verdict), and synchronization obligations (AM-34's two → zero). Every introduced rule is positive closed-set membership with no widen-on-new-form failure mode; a zero or positive recount at verification is refused and routed to The Eye.

## 7. Failure semantics and acceptance (LOAD-INV-6, LOAD-INV-10)

Constraints source §5 adopted verbatim: pre-execution validation; exact miss behavior with no normalization and no native fallback; no partial authority (one bad member invalidates the whole LAA); no partial evidence; no discovery repair (the gate may report the missing literal edge but may not scan source, suggest, or mutate declarations); version closure (no forward-compatible unknown-field acceptance); conflict routing to `terminal: needs-eye`. The full §6 acceptance suite is mandatory: every denial case uses an undeclared canary whose first action records an unmistakable side effect, and success requires the canary was never linked, instantiated, or executed — throwing after canary code runs is failure. Executable test records carry LAA digest, source digests, expected/actual results, and an assertion that rejected runs emitted no accepted evidence. DLC T7's concrete fixtures (illustrative-manifest F1, edge-drift F2 both directions, `Function('return import')()` F3, createRequire, undeclared side-effect import) are absorbed into the suite as adversarial examples, not a form inventory; completeness derives from the closed authority construction, not the fixture list.

## 8. Surfaced conflict for The Eye (not blended, not silently resolved)

CONFLICT-LDR-1: The implementation source asserts "RUNTIME: nothing. No hook, no interception" and counts any runtime mediation as loader machinery/runtime dependency; the constraints source (LOAD-INV-2/3, F3-generated, dynamic-import case) requires mediation of the semantic load boundary with source-pattern recognition ruled non-evidence — a purely static gate cannot prove a generated `import(s)` was denied at the boundary rather than recognized in text. This integration's resolution: the bounded compartment runs INSIDE the gate check, uses only Node ≥ 18 built-in `vm` primitives (zero runtime dependencies, no CLI loader flags, no `module.register`), and its per-unit target domain equals `u.imports` exactly — hence not a general-purpose loader under the constraints source's own definition, and not the ambient-interception machinery the implementation seat prohibited. If The Eye judges this to breach the implementation seat's zero-runtime-machinery frame, or if `vm.SourceTextModule` availability/behavior on the Node 18 floor cannot be proven (LOAD-INV-3 names Node-floor reliance failure a failed proof), status is `terminal: needs-eye`; the integrator will not resolve it by restoring the extractor-scanner, an export allowlist, subset semantics, or a runtime dependency.

## 9. Task decomposition (DLC §6 sequencing, retargeted)

T1 LAA schema + canonicalization/digest section (no deps). T2 AM-33 disposition table + confined/inert artifacts (joint sign-off with constraints seat). T3 bounded-compartment construction argument + Node-18-floor capability proof. T4 gate check `loader-authority` (registry entry, verdict/finding shape, fail-closed enumeration, byte-pinning discipline); deps T1–T3. T5 provenance rewiring (`orchestrator_refs` = LAA digest only; retire AM-34 text); dep T4. T6 retirement matrix + behavioral ledger recount; deps T1–T5. T7 full §6 acceptance suite incl. DLC fixtures, canary harness, atomicity proof; deps T1–T6.

obligation_matrix.invariant: Exactly one canonical, content-addressed LAA per weaver; graph, schema, runtime binding, locators, digests, and entry are canonical; only exact literal edges grant anything — missing, malformed, illustrative, wildcard, prefix, regex, default, or inferred permissions grant nothing, and nothing outside the addressed artifact can widen the graph.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Strict closed-schema validator + RFC 8785 JCS canonicalizer with NFC normalization, sorted units/imports, byte-compare of input vs canonical reserialization, external SHA-256 identity; unknown fields, duplicate JSON keys, and non-literal request values are schema-unrepresentable or rejected; the gate reads only the addressed artifact, so prose/config elsewhere is authority-inert.
obligation_matrix.task: T1 — LAA schema, canonical-path rules, JCS canonicalization and digest section, with canonicalization/digest/strict-rejection/graph-validation vector sets.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: F1 fixtures: submit an LAA with a prose-like top-level field ('permitted: relative imports, e.g. ./a.mjs'), a `request: "*"` edge, an omitted `imports` field, noncanonical byte order, duplicate JSON keys, and documentation-only extra permissions; each must be rejected as schema-invalid or grant nothing, and the undeclared canary must never link, instantiate, or execute.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: All canonicalization, digest, strict-rejection, and graph-validation vectors pass; every F1 fixture rejects fail-closed with canary unexecuted; evidence recorded that no artifact-external text widened any graph in the acceptance corpus.

obligation_matrix.invariant: Authority available to a load request from unit u EQUALS u.imports — every declared edge is an effective grant, every undeclared target is impossible to resolve or instantiate, and no native/package/URL/filesystem/ambient fallback runs after a declaration miss; authority is never determined by scanning source for loader forms.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Construction argument: the validated graph becomes the effective resolver domain — per-unit exact-literal edge maps drive both the vm.SourceTextModule link callback and importModuleDynamically inside the gate's bounded compartment; a miss throws RunInvalid before any resolution machinery is consulted; no AST walk, token search, regex, or import-form allowlist exists anywhere in the check (the DLC extractImportSurface routine is explicitly not built).
obligation_matrix.task: T3 + T4 — bounded-compartment construction-argument section and gate check `loader-authority` implementing the edge-map linker and dynamic-import handler over verified byte buffers.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: Positive/negative edge suite: for a representative cyclic and acyclic LAA, every declared edge (static, side-effect, and declared-dynamic request) resolves only to its mapped target's verified bytes; every undeclared request — including runtime-computed `import(value)`, undeclared side-effect import, file:/data:/absolute/bare/builtin specifiers — dies at the boundary with the canary never linked; additionally, repository evidence audit fails the build if any form-recognizer oracle is found in the check path.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: Construction argument accepted at verification; both directions of the edge suite pass (declared works, undeclared unlinkable); no fallback resolver invocation observable after any miss; zero form-recognition code paths in the check, confirmed by control-flow evidence.

obligation_matrix.invariant: A weaver unit is structurally unable to obtain a general-purpose loader: the transitive closure of initial compartment authority and everything reachable from it contains no resolver, loader, module namespace, host callback, global, service result, or reflective route whose target domain exceeds the caller's declared imports — including via generated code, aliases, and ambient Node routes — proven by positive authority construction, not a blacklist.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Positive compartment initialization: context contains only frozen inert ECMAScript intrinsics plus explicitly approved inert values/services per the §5 disposition; no require/module/process/host-loader callback present; node:module is not a possible module target kind so createRequire is unreachable; generated code either has codegen disabled or its import() routes through the same bounded per-unit map; service return values are inert data by construction; all on Node ≥ 18 ESM with zero runtime dependencies.
obligation_matrix.task: T3 — authority-reachability argument covering compartment initialization, imported namespaces, host callbacks, dynamic import, generated code, and service return values, plus the Node-18-floor capability proof for the vm primitives used.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: F3 fixtures: `new Function('s','return import(s)')('./undeclared.mjs')`; namespace/default/destructured/reassigned/multi-hop loader aliases including an attempted `createRequire` alias; global `require`, `module.require`, `createRequire(import.meta.url)`; process-mediated builtin acquisition and every available global/host callback probed for loader authority — in every case the canary must never link/instantiate/execute, and the oracle must be authority denial, not source-pattern detection.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: Authority-reachability argument accepted; all F3/require-style/ambient-acquisition fixtures pass with canary unexecuted and no pattern-oracle involved; Node-18-floor proof for every relied-on primitive passes (a floor-unavailable feature is a failed proof and routes to The Eye per CONFLICT-LDR-1).

obligation_matrix.invariant: The LAA is the sole source of both capability and provenance: recorded provenance is the validated LAA digest, its module projection is exactly entry + graph reachability, and no separately synthesized module list, source-derived closure, observed-load list, or independent provenance graph exists; a compatibility `orchestrator_refs` field may carry only the LAA digest.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: One validated immutable graph object feeds both the enforcement edge maps and provenance emission (the DLC one-artifact-two-reads model); `orchestrator_refs` schema is redefined to hold exactly the LAA digest, with copied module closures schema-invalid; consumers must dereference and validate the digest; edge mutation necessarily changes canonical bytes hence digest hence provenance identity.
obligation_matrix.task: T5 — provenance rewiring: emit the LAA digest as provenance, redefine `orchestrator_refs`, delete the separate closure derivation and AM-34 coupling text.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: F2 fixtures: validate LAA A, then submit a copied provenance module list and a mismatched digest B with one edge added/removed — copied list must be schema-invalid or ignored as non-authoritative, mismatched digest rejected; mutate one edge of A and assert the digest (and thus provenance identity) changes; attempt to feed enforcement and provenance from two different objects and show it is structurally impossible in the check's data flow.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: Single-object data flow demonstrated at verification (one graph object, two reads); all F2 fixtures reject; digest-changes-on-edge-mutation vector passes; no second derivation path exists per repository search and control-flow evidence.

obligation_matrix.invariant: The gate distrusts the submitted artifact and filesystem: it rereads the canonical LAA and every declared source from disk, validates paths (repo-relative canonical POSIX, within approved root, regular files, no symlink traversal, no duplicates) and hashes, and binds execution to those exact verified bytes — no reopen of mutable paths between validation and execution.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Byte-pinning discipline: symlink-refusing per-component lstat path walk under the approved root, regular-file check, single read into immutable buffers, sha256 verification against `sourceSha256`, and compartment module instantiation directly from those buffers so validation/execution byte identity holds by construction (TOCTOU race closed).
obligation_matrix.task: T4 — disk re-read and byte-pinning stage of the `loader-authority` gate check, including path-containment and duplicate-identity validation.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: Fixture set: mutate a declared file after validation but before execution; replace a declared file with a symlink; use a `..`/absolute/backslash path; declare duplicate paths or unit keys; submit a stale digest for changed bytes; run a validation/execution race harness attempting byte substitution — each must fail before any substituted bytes execute.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: All disk-mutation, symlink, traversal, duplicate-identity, stale-digest, and race fixtures reject before substituted bytes run; verification confirms execution consumes the pinned buffers (or an equivalent no-substitution proof) with no path reopen after validation.

obligation_matrix.invariant: Every failure class — unknown schema version, unknown property, noncanonical encoding, duplicate JSON keys, unsupported format, runtime mismatch, missing/unreachable units, duplicate requests, dangling targets, hash mismatch, undeclared request, unavailable confinement, provenance mismatch — rejects the run; no warning/permissive/auto-discovery/fallback mode exists; validation completes before any weaver unit executes; a post-start denial invalidates the entire run and none of its outputs are accepted.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Single fail-closed verdict pipeline in the gate check: ordered validation stages all complete before compartment execution begins; one enumerated failure taxonomy where the first violation yields {ok:false, finding} citing the violated obligation ID; run-record staging with publish-only-on-clean-verdict so a late denial atomically discards evidence, provenance, and success state; no code path reaches Node's ordinary resolver.
obligation_matrix.task: T4 — fail-closed failure enumeration and atomic run-invalidation staging in the `loader-authority` check; T7 — one negative fixture per failure class.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: One negative fixture per enumerated failure class (all thirteen+ classes listed in the invariant), each asserting rejection with the correct finding; plus the atomicity fixture: a run performing declared work, then hitting a late undeclared request — assert the entire run is invalid and no accepted artifact, evidence, or provenance was published.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: Complete per-class negative fixture coverage passes (coverage audited against the enumerated class list — no missing class); atomicity test proves rejected runs publish nothing; verification confirms absence of any warning/permissive/fallback branch.

obligation_matrix.invariant: No raw platform module or loader-capable namespace is exposed to a weaver to preserve non-loader functionality; every member of AM-33's exact frozen mapping receives exactly one disposition — preserved via a named confined artifact with tested equivalence, or removed with usage-absence evidence — with no e.g., ellipses, category stand-ins, or open remainder; unpreservable-without-loader-authority members route to The Eye.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Confined non-loader realization replacing the DLC BUILTIN_EXPORTS_PERMITTED grant model: inert generated-data units (e.g., builtin-name list as a hashed imports-empty ESM data unit; isBuiltin as inert-data membership lookup) inside the LAA graph, and/or compartment-injected inert service values whose returns are frozen plain data structurally unable to carry or manufacture loader authority.
obligation_matrix.task: T2 — complete AM-33 disposition table (one row per frozen-mapping member, joint sign-off with constraints seat), plus the named confined/inert artifacts with digests.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: For every preserved member: equivalence test comparing the confined artifact's result to the frozen expected behavior, PLUS an authority test proving the adapter cannot return or manufacture a loader/resolver/module namespace (probe return values, prototypes, and callbacks for loader reachability); for every removed member: repository evidence that no accepted Clotho behavior uses it; table-completeness audit fails on any e.g./ellipsis/category/remainder or on a member count mismatch against the frozen mapping.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: Disposition table is bijective with AM-33's frozen mapping and audit-clean; all equivalence and authority tests pass with artifact digests recorded; any unpreservable member is routed `terminal: needs-eye` rather than resolved by allowlist or scanner restoration.

obligation_matrix.invariant: All four old mechanisms — the loader-capability form scanner, LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS as permission mechanism, the source-derived accepted relative closure, and the shared-recognizer coupling — are removed or normatively superseded, with no old constant, branch, schema field, test oracle, documentation rule, or dormant fallback remaining as an independent authority path; historical forms survive only in black-box confinement tests that do not become a form definition.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Retirement matrix mapping each mechanism to deletion/schema replacement/demonstrated irrelevance (scanner → deleted, replaced by bounded-resolver semantics with no accepted-form extractor substituted; allowlist → deleted, members migrated per §5; separate closure → deleted, LAA graph sole closure; AM-34 → objectless, retired), backed by repository search evidence and control-flow evidence; adversarial test corpus declared non-normative for authority.
obligation_matrix.task: T6 — retirement matrix, spec v2.8 text strikes for AM-28/AM-31/AM-33/AM-34 machinery, repository search and control-flow evidence package.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: Orphan-path audit: repository-wide search for retired constants, scanner branches, old schema fields, allowlist references, and separate-closure code, failing on any hit outside black-box test fixtures; control-flow trace demonstrating no dormant fallback is reachable from the gate check; test-corpus audit asserting adversarial fixtures assert semantic confinement (canary non-execution) and contain no recognized-form oracle.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: Retirement matrix complete for all four mechanisms; search and control-flow evidence show zero orphan authority paths; test-corpus audit passes; verification confirms no old mechanism can influence any accept/reject decision.

obligation_matrix.invariant: Methodology-compatible behavioral-delta accounting against the four-mechanism baseline — counting semantic distinctions, validation branches, authority stores, failure modes, and synchronization obligations, not files or lines — shows the replacement model strictly smaller; renaming, relocating scanner logic, or omitting test-only branches is not reduction; zero or positive net is refused.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Before/after ledger recounted for the integrated model: 14 retired rules (7 scanner recognition/ambiguity/widening, 3 allowlist/AM-31/AM-33, 2 separate-derivation, 2 AM-34 coupling) vs 6 introduced positive closed-set rules (canonical schema+digest, byte-pinning, graph validation, exact-edge resolution, positive compartment bound, atomic invalidation) = net −8; authority stores 4→1; synchronization obligations 2→0; every introduced rule lacks a widen-on-new-form failure mode.
obligation_matrix.task: T6 — carry the recounted ledger into the plan delta with each removed and introduced distinction individually identified and cross-referenced to the retirement matrix.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: Ledger audit at verification: independently recount distinctions from the integrated spec text and the retired spec text; the audit fails if any retired rule survives under a new name (cross-check against LOAD-INV-8 evidence), if scanner logic reappears inside the validator or compartment, if test-only branches were omitted from the count, or if the net total is ≥ 0.
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: Independent recount confirms a strictly negative net (−8 as tabled, or corrected but still strictly negative); a zero/positive result is refused and routed to The Eye; ledger accepted into the plan delta.

obligation_matrix.invariant: The complete §6 acceptance suite is mandatory: every declared target works, every undeclared canary remains unlinked, uninstantiated, and unexecuted (throwing after unauthorized code runs is failure), and executable test records carry LAA digest, source digests, expected/actual results, and an assertion that rejected runs emitted no accepted evidence.
obligation_matrix.mechanism: Canary-instrumented executable acceptance harness implementing all §6 cases (F1 illustrative-set, F2 drift, F3 generated and aliased, require-style, dynamic import, side-effect import, generated-artifact/URL, ambient acquisition, alias/path substitution, declared-graph positive on cyclic and acyclic LAAs, legitimate safe functionality per §5 table, atomic rejection); the canary's first action records an unmistakable side effect so link/instantiate/execute status is directly observable; run records embed digests and the no-accepted-evidence assertion; the fixture set is declared adversarial examples, with completeness grounded in the LOAD-INV-2/3 constructions.
obligation_matrix.task: T7 — full acceptance suite build, canary harness, executable test-record format, and atomicity proof, absorbing the DLC T7 must-defeat fixtures.
obligation_matrix.negative_test: The suite itself is the adversarial battery; its meta-negative tests: assert every denial case's canary side-effect channel recorded nothing (a throw-after-execution result is scored as suite FAILURE); assert every rejected run's record contains the no-accepted-evidence assertion and no published provenance; assert every record carries the LAA digest and all source digests (a record missing any field fails the suite).
obligation_matrix.exit_criterion: All §6 cases pass with declared targets working, zero canary executions across all denial cases, and complete well-formed test records archived; any case failure, canary execution, or record deficiency blocks acceptance and — if it exposes a frame conflict (e.g., CONFLICT-LDR-1) — routes `terminal: needs-eye`.
